steps.title: Recall the vertical shift rule
steps.content:
To shift a graph up by $k$ units, add $k$ to the output of the function:
$$g(x)=f(x)+k$$

steps.title: Test each choice
steps.content:
• Choice A — $g(x)=3x^{5}+9$: $g(x)=3x^5+9$: adds 9 to the output — a vertical shift up by 9. ✓
  • Choice B — $g(x)=3x^{5}-9$: $g(x)=3x^5-9$: subtracts 9, which shifts down by 9. Eliminate.
  • Choice C — $g(x)=3x^{45}$: $g(x)=3x^{45}$: changes the exponent — not a shift at all. Eliminate.
  • Choice D — $g(x)=27x^{5}$: $g(x)=27x^5$: a vertical stretch, not a shift. Eliminate.
That is the correct answer.
